    BookCover Agent Portal
    AI-Powered Member Retention for Medicare Advantage Carriers · cercalabs.com
    Carrier Retention Demo
    What is the BookCover Agent Portal?

    The BookCover Agent Portal is a retention management system built for Medicare Advantage carrier retention teams. It combines member-level risk intelligence with automated outreach campaign management — enabling agents to proactively engage at-risk members before they switch plans during AEP.

    The system does the analytical heavy lifting. Agents spend 30–60 minutes per day reviewing dashboards, approving system-generated campaigns, and following up with members flagged for outreach — not building targeting lists or writing emails from scratch.

    📊
    Retention Scoring
    0–100 risk score per member, updated daily from FHIR claims and engagement data — identifies who is at risk months before AEP
    📧
    Retention CampaignsTHIS DEMO
    System-generated, agent-approved email and Facebook campaigns targeting the right members with the right message at the right time
    👤
    Member ManagementTHIS DEMO
    Full 1:1 member view — score factors, claims history, communications timeline, agent notes, and personal outreach tools
    📈
    Retention ReportingTHIS DEMO
    Analytics dashboards and automated report delivery to management, including the AEP Stay vs Switch AEP analysis for all MA and Dual members

    AEP Stay vs Switch analysis is a flagship deliverable — run every September, it compares OOPC across all 2026 plans for every MA and Dual member, generating Stay or Switch recommendations with physician network verification before AEP outreach begins.
    About This Demo

    This demo shows the agent-facing portal for a fictional carrier, Your Health Plan, managing a book of 12,450 Medicare Advantage members. The agent is Sandra K. All data is representative — member scores, campaign batches, and report figures reflect realistic operating conditions.

    Four Use Case Flows
    1
    Agent's Daily ActivityDashboard review → at-risk members → approve campaign → member outreach
    2
    Campaign WorkflowCampaign list → batch review → edit email → delivery settings → approved
    3
    Member DetailsFilter at-risk → score factors → comms timeline → log outreach & reminder
    4
    Reporting & SharingReports dashboard → AEP Stay vs Switch analysis → compose email → recurring schedule

    Why It Works
    How BookCover’s combination of features drives retention · a philosophical deep-dive
    About BookCover
    The retention thesis

    Retention isn’t a campaign.
    It’s a relationship.

    Members don’t shop for insurance because they want to. They shop because they don’t feel seen. BookCover gives retention teams the operational layer to fix that — quietly, continuously, and with claims data behind every touchpoint.

    Why members leave

    Disenrollment correlates more strongly with the absence of meaningful contact than with price, benefits, or even bad service experiences. Most members hear from their carrier twice a year — at enrollment and at renewal. The 50 weeks in between are silent.

    By the time a member is comparing plans online, the relationship has already failed.

    What real retention requires

    Three things high-retention industries practice daily, and insurance usually doesn’t:

    Continuous contact
    Showing up between transactions, not just during them.
    Real personalization
    Not “Dear [First Name].” Knowing what care a member actually uses, what they pay, and what’s changing.
    Recognition of the relationship
    Members aren’t accounts. They’re people navigating one of the most consequential purchases of their adult lives.

    The reason these are missing in insurance isn’t carrier indifference — it’s operational. Twelve thousand members and twenty agents will always lose to scale. BookCover changes the math.

    What BookCover does

    01
    Continuous engagement, automated.
    Touchpoints land on the right cadence, with the right message, for the right segment — without a campaign manager hand-building each one. The work scales with members, not with headcount.
    02
    Personalization from claims data, not surveys.
    Every interaction is shaped by what the member actually uses — their doctors, their prescriptions, their utilization, their costs. Generic outreach gives way to genuinely individual messaging. The member feels recognized because they are.
    03
    Quantitative risk scoring with full visibility.
    Every member has a retention risk score that’s interpretable, current, and actionable. Retention teams can sort, filter, and prioritize their book by actual signal — not gut feel.

    How the risk score works

    The score combines three signal categories that update in real time:

    Standard risk factors
    Demographics, plan type, tenure, premium relative to market, network coverage of the member’s existing providers.
    Member behavior
    Claims patterns, utilization trends, channel engagement, switching history, life-stage triggers (Medicare eligibility, change of address, new diagnosis).
    Carrier behavior
    Premium changes, network changes, formulary changes, service quality signals. Members react to what the carrier does — not only to what the member does.
    Member voice (survey responses)
    Direct answers from members on plan satisfaction, switching intent, customer service experience, and network adequacy. Survey responses refine risk scores for respondents and surface concerns the analytical signals miss — like a member who looks healthy on paper but has been frustrated by three unanswered service calls.

    The result is a score agents can read like a chart: which factors moved, by how much, and what to do about each. Risk isn’t a number to hide behind. It’s a map to act on.

    From insight to action

    Two analytics tools turn the score into specific work agents can do today:

    Transition Reports
    Members aging out of IFP into Medicare are identified months in advance, with their physician-match analysis and projected costs across plan options ready before the conversation begins. No member gets caught off-guard. No agent walks into the discussion unprepared.
    Stay-Switch Analysis
    During AEP, every member gets a clear recommendation — Stay or Switch — grounded in physician match and projected out-of-pocket cost from prior claims. Not an abstract score. Just: this plan, this cost, these doctors.

    Both feed directly into pre-drafted campaigns, personalized to the member’s actual situation.

    What this looks like to the member

    —An October email confirming their current plan is still right for them, with the specific reasons why. Not a generic enrollment reminder.
    —A call months before Medicare eligibility, with new plan options already mapped to their existing doctors and prescriptions. Not a frantic last-minute scramble.
    —A timely notice when something in their care relationships changes, with a clear next step. Not a delayed reaction to a problem they’ve already had.

    Members don’t shop because they want to. They shop because they feel forgotten.

    BookCover makes sure they aren’t.
